title: The Team
text:
The Accelerating Clinical Trials – Clinical Trials Unit (ACT-CTU) brings together clinical, scientific, and operational expertise to support the design and delivery of high-quality clinical trials at the RI-MUHC.








Co-Chair, ACT-CTU
Dr. Sushmita Pamidi is a Respirologist and Sleep Physician, and an Associate Professor in the Department of Medicine at McGill University and the Research Institute of the McGill University Health Centre (RI-MUHC). She is a clinician-scientist with expertise in epidemiology, clinical trials, and health outcomes research, using both observational and interventional study designs.
Dr. Pamidi completed her medical training at the University of Toronto, residency in Internal Medicine and Respirology at the University of Western Ontario, and fellowship training in Sleep Medicine at the University of Chicago. She subsequently completed a Master’s in Epidemiology at McGill University, where she now leads an active research program. She is Director of the Respiratory Epidemiology and Clinical Research Unit and Co-Leader of the Translational Research in Respiratory Diseases Program. Her research focuses on cardiometabolic health and obstructive sleep apnea, including its impact on high-risk populations such as pregnant individuals.






Co-Chair, ACT-CTU
Dr. Louise Pilote is a Senior Scientist at the Research Institute of the McGill University Health Centre (RI-MUHC) and a Professor of Medicine at McGill University. She serves as Deputy Executive Director and Deputy Chief Scientific Officer of the RI-MUHC and holds a James McGill Chair.
Dr. Pilote completed a Master of Public Health at the Harvard School of Public Health and a PhD in Epidemiology at the University of California, Berkeley. A clinician-scientist by training, her research focuses on cardiovascular epidemiology, health services research, and outcomes research. She has published extensively on the effectiveness and quality of cardiovascular care, with an emphasis on improving patient outcomes and equity in care delivery.






Project Manager, ACT-CTU
Amanda Lovato is the Project Manager of the Accelerating Clinical Trials – Clinical Trials Unit (ACT-CTU) at the Research Institute of the McGill University Health Centre (RI-MUHC). In this role, she leads the development and coordination of institutional initiatives designed to strengthen clinical trial capacity, support investigators, and foster a collaborative clinical research ecosystem.
Amanda plays a central role in advancing ACT-CTU programs, including Clinical Trial Rounds, Protocol Review Sessions, regulatory and methodological support services, and national collaboration initiatives. She works closely with investigators, institutional leadership, and national partners to enhance trial infrastructure, streamline processes, and support the successful delivery of clinical research across the RI-MUHC and the broader ACT network.

title: Protocol Review Sessions
text:
The ACT-CTU offers Protocol Review Sessions — a structured, expert-led forum designed to strengthen your clinical trial at a critical stage of development.
These sessions provide investigators with high-value, real-time feedback from experienced trialists, methodologists, and clinicians, helping to refine study design, improve feasibility, and enhance competitiveness for funding.

title: ACT-CTU Protocol Review Sessions
text:
ACT-CTU Protocol Review Sessions
The ACT-CTU Protocol Review Sessions provide investigators with structured, expert feedback on clinical trial design, feasibility, and implementation. Each session brings together multidisciplinary panels to strengthen study rigor and optimize trial success.

title: Accelerating Clinical Trials – Clinical Trials Unit (ACT-CTU)
text:
The Accelerating Clinical Trials – Clinical Trials Unit (ACT-CTU) at the Research Institute of the McGill University Health Centre (RI-MUHC) is dedicated to strengthening clinical trial capacity, accelerating study start-up and delivery, and fostering a collaborative and inclusive clinical research ecosystem.
Through strategic initiatives, shared expertise, and national partnerships, the ACT-CTU supports investigators across disciplines in designing and conducting high-quality, impactful clinical trials.

title: Accelerating Clinical Trials – Clinical Trials Unit (ACT-CTU)
text:
Our Unit
The ACT-CTU was established to address critical gaps in clinical trial infrastructure and support at the RI-MUHC. By providing centralized expertise, developing shared resources, and connecting researchers with national networks, the unit enables the successful execution of trials across a wide range of therapeutic areas.
Key areas of focus include:

Supporting trial design, methodology, and regulatory navigation

Enhancing patient recruitment and engagement strategies

Building institutional infrastructure and shared services

Facilitating collaboration across the national ACT network

title: Accelerating Clinical Trials – Clinical Trials Unit (ACT-CTU)
text:
What We Do
The ACT-CTU leads and supports a diverse portfolio of initiatives designed to strengthen the clinical trials ecosystem at the RI-MUHC.
These include:

Clinical Trial Rounds and Protocol Review Sessions

Deployment of shared regulatory and methodological expertise

Development of institutional trial data resources

Implementation of clinical trial infrastructure (CTMS, eTMF)

Pilot funding programs to seed future trials

Patient engagement and inclusive recruitment strategies

title: Internal Call for Pilot Studies
text:
The ACT-CTU Internal Pilot Trials Program supports the development of high-quality, investigator-initiated clinical trials at the RI-MUHC.
Each selected project receives:

$50,000 in funding per trial
Methodological and operational support from the ACT-CTU
Access to institutional and national clinical trial expertise

This initiative is designed to seed innovative studies and position investigators for larger-scale funding opportunities.

title: Internal Calls for Pilot Studies
text:
Internal Pilot Trials Program
The ACT-CTU Internal Pilot Trials Program supports the development of high-quality, investigator-initiated clinical trials at the RI-MUHC.
Each selected project receives:

$50,000 in funding per trial
Methodological and operational support from the ACT-CTU
Access to institutional and national clinical trial expertise

This initiative is designed to seed innovative studies and position investigators for larger-scale funding opportunities.

title: Internal Calls for Pilot Studies
text:
Program Impact
The ACT-CTU Pilot Trials Program has demonstrated strong demand and impact, with increasing application volume and successful progression of funded studies toward larger-scale trials and external funding.
This initiative plays a critical role in strengthening the clinical trials pipeline at the RI-MUHC and fostering innovation across programs.

title: ACT Network Collaborations
section: Expand Your Trial’s Reach Through the ACT Network
text:
Expand Your Trial’s Reach Through the ACT Network
The ACT-CTU supports investigators in expanding the reach of their clinical trials through the national ACT Consortium, enabling recruitment across a network of community (portfolio) hospitals across Canada.

title: ACT Network Collaborations
section: Recruit Patients Through ACT Portfolio Hospitals
text:
Recruit Patients Through ACT Portfolio Hospitals
If you are seeking to recruit participants for your randomized controlled trial through community hospitals within the ACT Consortium, you can express your interest using the short submission form below.
👉 Submit your trial for consideration (5-minute form)

title: ACT Network Collaborations
text:
Explore Ongoing ACT-Affiliated Trials
If you are interested in collaborating on or learning more about existing trials within the ACT network, please consult the current list of active ACT-supported trials:
👉 View the current ACT Trials Portfolio

title: ACT Network Collaborations
section: About the ACT Portfolio Hospital Program
text:
About the ACT Portfolio Hospital Program
The ACT Portfolio Hospital Program supports the conduct of high-quality randomized controlled trials (RCTs) by providing dedicated research coordination support across a network of community hospitals in Canada.
This program funds Research Coordinators in 20 community hospitals nationwide, enabling investigators to: - Expand recruitment beyond academic centres
- Increase trial feasibility and reach
- Strengthen equity and diversity in participant enrolment
👉 View participating portfolio hospitals (PDF)

title: ACT Network Collaborations
text:
Why This Matters
By leveraging the ACT Network, investigators can: - Accelerate recruitment timelines
- Improve access to diverse patient populations
- Enhance trial feasibility and generalizability
- Strengthen national collaboration and impact
The ACT-CTU is available to support investigators in connecting with portfolio hospitals and navigating ACT-supported trial pathways.

title: Education
text:
The ACT-CTU supports education and capacity building in clinical trials through training initiatives, workshops, and collaborative learning opportunities.

title: Trial Methods Support
text:
The ACT-CTU provides access to specialized methodological expertise to support the design, optimization, and implementation of high-quality clinical trials.
Through the Novel Trial Methods Hub, investigators are connected with experienced methodologists to strengthen study design, improve feasibility, and enhance the likelihood of funding success.

title: Trial Methods Support
text:
The ACT-CTU provides access to specialized methodological expertise to support the design, optimization, and implementation of high-quality clinical trials.
Through the Novel Trial Methods Hub, investigators are connected with experienced methodologists to strengthen study design, improve feasibility, and enhance the likelihood of funding success.

title: Trial Methods Support
section: The ACT-CTU Novel Trial Methods Hub
text:
The ACT-CTU Novel Trial Methods Hub
The Novel Trial Methods Hub is a centralized, multidisciplinary platform designed to support investigators in developing next-generation clinical trials. :contentReferenceoaicite:0

What the Hub Offers

Early-stage trial design support (prior to grant submission)

Protocol optimization to improve feasibility and efficiency

Access to advanced and innovative methodologies, including:

Adaptive and platform trials

Pragmatic and real-world trials

Decentralized and digital trials

Bayesian and advanced statistical methods

Innovative endpoints and biomarkers


Support to strengthen grant competitiveness and trial success

The Hub enables investigators to move from promising ideas to fundable, future-ready trials.


Types of Support Available

Protocol and Statistical Analysis Plan development

Adaptive and Bayesian trial design

Feasibility and pilot trial design

Early-phase and innovative trial methodologies

Real-world evidence integration

External control and single-arm trial design

Covariate adjustment and subgroup analysis

Estimands framework integration

title: Trial Methods Support
text:
Request Trial Methods Support
Investigators seeking methodological and/or statistical support from the ACT-CTU Novel Trial Methods Hub are invited to complete the intake form below.
👉 Download the Methods Hub Intake Form
Please complete the form and submit it by email to:
📧 amanda.lovato@rimuhc.ca

title: Trial Methods Support
text:
Submission Instructions

Download the intake form

Complete all relevant sections

Attach the completed document to an email

Send to amanda.lovato@rimuhc.ca with the subject line:

“Methods Hub Request – [Your Name]”

title: Trial Methods Support
section: Early engagement with the Methods Hub allows us to provide more effective support and optimize your study design prior to funding submission.
text: Early engagement with the Methods Hub allows us to provide more effective support and optimize your study design prior to funding submission.

text:

Amanda Lovato

title: Trial Methods Support
text:
Why This Matters
High-quality trial design is critical to success.
By engaging early with the Methods Hub, investigators can: - Improve study feasibility and efficiency
- Strengthen methodological rigor
- Increase competitiveness for funding
- Enhance the impact of their research
The ACT-CTU is committed to supporting investigators at every stage of trial development.

title: Protocol Review Sessions
text:
The ACT-CTU offers Protocol Review Sessions — a structured, expert-led forum designed to strengthen your clinical trial at a critical stage of development.
These sessions provide investigators with high-value, real-time feedback from experienced trialists, methodologists, and clinicians, helping to refine study design, improve feasibility, and enhance competitiveness for funding.

title: Protocol Review Sessions
section: What is a Protocol Review Session?
text:
What is a Protocol Review Session?
A Protocol Review Session is a 1-hour live, interactive feedback forum structured as follows:

10-minute presentation by the Principal Investigator
(background, objectives, study design, protocol, outcomes)

3–5 key questions posed by the PI to guide discussion

50-minute expert panel discussion and audience feedback

Sessions are: - Moderated by the ACT-CTU
- Tailored to your study and key challenges
- Open to the research community
- Promoted through ACT-CTU communications

title: Protocol Review Sessions
text:
Why Participate?
Protocol Review Sessions provide a unique opportunity to:

Strengthen your study design before submission

Identify methodological and feasibility challenges early

Improve grant competitiveness

Receive input from leading clinical trialists and methodologists

Build connections with potential collaborators and long-term partners

Contribute to and benefit from a learning-oriented research community

title: Protocol Review Sessions
text:
Request a Protocol Review Session
If you are developing a clinical trial and would benefit from structured expert feedback, we invite you to request a session.
📧 Request a Protocol Review Session

title: Protocol Review Sessions
section: What We Will Need From You
text:
What We Will Need From You
To organize your session, we will ask for:

A copy of your latest protocol or research plan

3–5 key questions you would like feedback on

Your availability and timeline

Based on your project and questions, the ACT-CTU will: - Identify and invite relevant expert panelists
- Coordinate logistics and promotion
- Facilitate the session

title: Protocol Review Sessions
section: Join as a Reviewer or Expert Panelist
text:
Join as a Reviewer or Expert Panelist
We are continuously building a network of experts interested in contributing to protocol development and advancing clinical trial excellence.
If you would like to: - Serve as a panelist
- Provide methodological or clinical feedback
- Engage with emerging trial designs
📧 Express interest in becoming a reviewer

title: Protocol Review Sessions
text:
A Collaborative Learning Environment
Beyond supporting individual studies, Protocol Review Sessions are designed to foster a culture of shared learning, methodological rigor, and collaboration across the clinical trials community.

title: REB Submission Support
text:
Preparing a strong REB submission is critical to avoiding delays and ensuring a smooth study start-up.
The ACT-CTU offers pre-submission review of your Nagano package to help identify gaps, improve clarity, and strengthen the overall quality of your submission before it is reviewed by the Research Ethics Board (REB).

title: REB Submission Support
text:
Preparing a strong REB submission is critical to avoiding delays and ensuring a smooth study start-up.
The ACT-CTU offers pre-submission review of your Nagano package to help identify gaps, improve clarity, and strengthen the overall quality of your submission before it is reviewed by the Research Ethics Board (REB).

title: REB Submission Support
text:
What We Offer
Our team provides structured feedback on your Nagano submission package, including:

Completeness and internal consistency

Clarity of study objectives and design

Alignment between protocol, consent, and supporting documents

Identification of common REB concerns and potential delays

Overall readiness for submission

This review is designed to help you anticipate reviewer questions and reduce back-and-forth during ethics review.

title: REB Submission Support
text:
Why This Matters
A well-prepared Nagano submission can:

Reduce REB review timelines

Minimize requests for revisions

Improve clarity for reviewers

Accelerate study start-up

Early feedback can make a meaningful difference in both efficiency and success.

title: REB Submission Support
text:
Request a Pre-Submission Review
Investigators are invited to submit their Nagano package for review through the RI-MUHC Research Facilitator.
📧 Request Nagano Pre-Submission Review

title: REB Submission Support
text:
What to Include
To facilitate review, please include:

Your completed Nagano submission package

Any supporting documents (protocol, consent forms, etc.)

Your target submission timeline

title: REB Submission Support
text:
Dedicated Support
This service is coordinated by the RI-MUHC Research Facilitator:
Josie Campisi
Research Facilitator, RI-MUHC
📧 research.facilitator@muhc.mcgill.ca

title: REB Submission Support
section: A More Efficient Path to Ethics Approval
text:
A More Efficient Path to Ethics Approval
By engaging early with this service, investigators can strengthen their submission, reduce delays, and move more efficiently from study concept to activation.

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Preparing a strong Health Canada submission can be complex, time-sensitive, and critical to trial activation. The ACT-CTU offers access to specialized regulatory support to help investigators navigate submission requirements with greater confidence and efficiency.
Through this service, investigators may request a consultation with Lori Anderson, an experienced regulatory consultant with expertise in Health Canada submissions.

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Preparing a strong Health Canada submission can be complex, time-sensitive, and critical to trial activation. The ACT-CTU offers access to specialized regulatory support to help investigators navigate submission requirements with greater confidence and efficiency.
Through this service, investigators may request a consultation with Lori Anderson, an experienced regulatory consultant with expertise in Health Canada submissions.

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
What This Service Offers
This service is designed to support investigators who require guidance with Health Canada regulatory processes for clinical trials.
Support may include:

Guidance on Health Canada submission requirements

Review of submission strategy and documentation

Advice on preparing and organizing regulatory packages

Input on common challenges and areas requiring clarification

Support to strengthen readiness prior to submission

This service is intended to help investigators reduce avoidable delays and improve the quality and completeness of their submission package.

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Consultant
Lori Anderson
Regulatory Consultant
Expertise: Health Canada clinical trial submissions

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Fees
Consultations are available at a rate of $100 CAD per hour.
Please note:

Fees are the responsibility of the Principal Investigator
Consultation time and scope can be tailored to project needs

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Request a Consultation
Investigators interested in this service are invited to contact the ACT-CTU to request a consultation.
📧 Request a Health Canada Submission Consultation

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
section: What to Include in Your Request
text:
What to Include in Your Request
To help us triage your request efficiently, please include:

Principal Investigator name

Project title

Brief description of the study

Type of support requested

Relevant timelines or submission deadline

Any specific questions or challenges you would like to address

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Why This Matters
Health Canada submissions are a critical component of trial start-up. Early expert input can help investigators:

Clarify submission requirements

Strengthen submission quality

Anticipate regulatory questions

Improve efficiency and reduce delays

The ACT-CTU is committed to helping investigators access the expertise needed to advance high-quality clinical trials at the RI-MUHC.

title: Health Canada Regulatory Submission Support
text:
Get Started
If you would like to explore whether this service is appropriate for your study, please reach out:
📧 Contact us to request a consultation
